title: Region-Specific Calibration Achieves Excellent Inter-Device Reliability for Smartphone Dermatology
established: Matched tri-device imaging; global and regional affine CCM; anatomical region affects transfer.
limitations: DSLR is not spectral truth; processed sRGB/JPEG; illumination/device effects confounded; ICC can overstate agreement; no spectrum recovery.
preempted_claim: generic novelty of region-specific smartphone skin-color calibration
admissible_extension: Explain residual error via measured spectral observability; quantify capture-control boundary.

title: Limitations of Data-Driven Spectral Reconstruction: An Optics-Aware Analysis
established: RGB-to-spectrum inversion is non-unique; benchmark shortcuts and cross-dataset collapse; wavelength-dependent PSF can encode extra information.
limitations: Generic/natural-scene metamers may be physiologically implausible; information gain from aberration not fully quantified.
preempted_claim: generic claim that RGB spectral reconstruction is ill-posed; chromatic aberration as side channel
admissible_extension: Enumerate real-skin collisions; constrain metamers to measured skin manifold; quantify added rank and posterior contraction.

title: Hyper-Skin: A Hyperspectral Dataset for Reconstructing Facial Skin-Spectra from RGB Images
established: Facial skin hyperspectral dataset; paired synthetic RGB; RGB-to-VIS and RGB+NIR-to-NIR benchmarks.
limitations: Synthetic RGB does not reproduce full consumer optical/RAW/ISP chain; controlled cohort; prior dependence.
preempted_claim: skin-specific HSI dataset and synthetic RGB-to-spectrum benchmark novelty
admissible_extension: Device/illuminant holdout; evidence-versus-prior decomposition; real RAW paired acquisition.

title: CleanPatrick: A Benchmark for Image Data Cleaning
established: Large-scale audit of off-topic images, near duplicates, and label errors in Fitzpatrick17k.
limitations: Cleaning labels are judgment-dependent and may be tone/disease dependent; no causal performance decomposition.
preempted_claim: novelty of discovering Fitzpatrick17k quality defects
admissible_extension: Quantify disparity change under each cleaning layer and differential removal by tone/disease.

title: Diverse Dermatology Images: A Benchmark for Dermatology AI
established: Pathology-confirmed diverse clinical-image benchmark; performance degradation on darker skin and uncommon disease.
limitations: Does not identify optical cause; tone labels are not spectral truth; disease mix, contrast, quality, and domain can confound.
preempted_claim: novelty of demonstrating a dermatology performance gap
admissible_extension: Joint physics/contrast/data-quality decomposition; independent replication in DDI-2.

title: Evaluating Deep Neural Networks with the Fitzpatrick17k Dataset
established: Large atlas-derived dataset with Fitzpatrick labels and subgroup performance benchmarking.
limitations: Fitzpatrick is phenotype, not continuous measured color; provenance, labels, and duplicates are problematic.
admissible_extension: Raw-versus-cleaned disparity decomposition; avoid physical claims.

title: Monk Skin Tone Examples / MST-E
established: Image/video examples across Monk Skin Tone categories and capture conditions.
limitations: MST is perceptual/ordinal; repeated media create leakage; small subject count.
admissible_extension: LOSO capture instability analysis tied to ISSA-derived recoverability.

title: Smartphone Tristimulus Colorimetry for Skin-Tone Analysis
established: Under controlled geometry, illumination, exposure, and calibration, smartphone tristimulus estimates can approach colorimeter agreement.
limitations: Small cohort; controlled protocol; does not establish spectral or chromophore recovery.
preempted_claim: categorical claim that smartphones cannot measure skin color accurately
admissible_extension: Quantify minimum control required by target, signal level, device, and appearance.

title: Standardized Spectral and Radiometric Calibration of Consumer Cameras
established: Practical consumer-camera spectral and radiometric calibration framework.
limitations: Calibration does not prove processed RGB contains sufficient information; device-specific.
admissible_extension: Propagate calibration uncertainty into recoverability and establish minimum sufficient calibration.

title: Spectral Sensitivity Estimation Without a Camera
established: Camera spectral sensitivities can be estimated from metadata and learned priors.
limitations: Estimated curves are prior-dependent and not equivalent to direct monochromator measurement.
preempted_claim: novelty of estimating camera sensitivity from public metadata
admissible_extension: Measured-versus-estimated operator sensitivity and uncertainty propagation.

title: Polarisation Optics for Biomedical and Clinical Applications: A Review
established: Polarization can suppress surface reflection and modify depth/structural sensitivity.
limitations: General biomedical review; geometry/motion burden; not proven optimal for this target.
preempted_claim: novelty of adding polarization to skin imaging
admissible_extension: Compare information gain against NIR, multi-flash, RAW, reference patch, and repeats under equal cost.

title: A Practical Model for Skin Reflectance and Biophysical Parameter Estimation
established: Layered skin reflectance modeling and inversion for biophysical parameters.
limitations: Fit quality does not imply unique parameter recovery; results depend on priors, ranges, geometry, and calibration.
preempted_claim: novelty of layered skin optical modeling or generic biophysical inversion
admissible_extension: Fit across ISSA strata; model discrepancy; structural/practical identifiability; separate fit from truth.

title: Computational Spectral Reconstruction Methods from RGB to Hyperspectral Imaging: A Review
established: Taxonomy of prior-based, sparse, regression, and deep RGB-to-HSI reconstruction methods.
limitations: Does not resolve skin-specific observability or measurement-versus-prior attribution.
preempted_claim: architecture-family novelty without solving a registered physical gap
admissible_extension: No architecture novelty claim unless it closes a measured physical/inferential gap.

title: A Reference Data Set for Human Skin Reflectance
established: Traceable human-skin reflectance reference data and measurement conditions.
limitations: Smaller scope; no arbitrary consumer-image pairs.
preempted_claim: novelty of publishing a basic skin-reflectance reference
admissible_extension: Cross-dataset spectral geometry and model-discrepancy replication.

title: Hyperspectral Imaging Database of Human Facial Skin
established: 29 faces; 400–720 nm at 10 nm; nine facial regions; comparison with TSR and contact spectrophotometer; local spatial variation.
limitations: Small cohort; only four darker-tone participants; facial-only; substantial device/geometry disagreement.
preempted_claim: novelty of facial hyperspectral mapping and anatomical-location variation
admissible_extension: Test ISSA manifold/observability findings spatially and locally; treat point averages as sampling operators.

title: Estimating Skin Tone and Effects on Classification Performance in Dermatology Datasets
established: Image-derived ITA from non-lesional skin; darker appearance underrepresented; no measurable performance trend in studied setting.
limitations: ITA and segmentation are image-derived; dark-end sample is weak for strong null inference.
admissible_extension: Use instrument-grounded predictions and powered dark-end data; accept null clinical bridge.

title: Eigenspectra Optoacoustic Tomography
established: Low-dimensional eigenspectra compensate wavelength-dependent fluence in optoacoustic imaging; multi-level validation.
limitations: Different modality and physics; cannot validate passive visible-reflectance RGB recovery.
preempted_claim: generic novelty of empirical spectral bases for tissue-light interaction
admissible_extension: Visible-reflectance-specific basis analysis with direct metrology and camera operators.

title: Physically Plausible Spectral Reconstruction / NTIRE 2020 Spectral Reconstruction Challenge
established: Large clean and real-world RGB-to-HSI benchmark; strong deep methods; reprojection and computational limitations.
limitations: Natural scenes, synthetic clean RGB, unknown real-world processing; metrics can reward physically inconsistent spectra.
preempted_claim: generic multi-network RGB2HS benchmarking and generic physical-consistency testing
admissible_extension: Skin-specific physical consistency, evidence/prior accounting, external device/illuminant holdout.
